title: Harvest Festival

jokes.text: And it is possibly the best thing to potentially ever happen to anyone anywhere in the history of the Universe.
jokes.setup: Leslie announces she has a surprise for everyone
jokes.punchline: The absurdly grandiose buildup for what turns out to be a mini horse
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie hyperbole that perfectly establishes character and sets up the Little Sebastian reveal. The escalation is genuinely funny and character-specific.

jokes.text: Sebastian was the number one boy's name in Pawnee. And the number three girl's name.
jokes.setup: Explaining Little Sebastian's cultural impact on the town
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail that Sebastian was also a popular girl's name
jokes.explanation: Economical joke that builds the world's obsession with Little Sebastian in an unexpected way.

jokes.text: So what am I missing? What's the deal with the pony?
jokes.setup: Everyone is excited about Little Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Ben's complete bewilderment at the town's obsession
jokes.explanation: Sets up Ben's running confusion about Little Sebastian, perfect character moment.

jokes.text: He's not a pony, man. He's a mini horse. There's a big difference.
jokes.setup: Ben calls Little Sebastian a pony
jokes.punchline: Tom's serious correction as if it matters tremendously
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the town's obsessive devotion to Little Sebastian.

jokes.text: Son, this horse has an honorary degree from Notre Dame.
jokes.setup: Ben asking what makes Little Sebastian special
jokes.punchline: Ron's deadpan delivery of an absurd credential
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson delivery of absurd information as if it's completely reasonable. High quotability.

jokes.text: We are both on the same diabetes medication. Are you my Glucotrol buddy? Are you? Huh?
jokes.setup: Jerry is assigned to watch Little Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Jerry's awkward bonding attempt with the horse over shared medical issues
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry awkwardness, perfectly in character and genuinely uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Get out.
jokes.setup: Ben admits he doesn't understand the big deal about Little Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate, serious banishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and escalation. Leslie's extreme reaction is both shocking and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Well, there's a lot of people that don't consider salad a food.
jokes.setup: Discussion about placing Sue's salads among junk food vendors
jokes.punchline: Ron's anti-vegetable philosophy delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron Swanson anti-health food sentiment delivered perfectly in character.

jokes.text: God, I gotta stop ending on that boring thing. Clowns!
jokes.setup: Leslie realizes she ended her pitch on a boring note
jokes.punchline: Her immediate self-correction with 'Clowns!' as if that's more exciting
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's self-awareness and compulsive need to end on a high note.

jokes.text: You're like a ninja crossed with a Jedi, or something. / You're like a nerd mixed with a dork or something.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to compliment Tom
jokes.punchline: Tom's immediate counter-insult using Ben's own format
jokes.explanation: Simple back-and-forth that establishes the Ben-Tom dynamic but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Ah! Wahallo.
jokes.setup: Ken Hotate enters the scene
jokes.punchline: His overly theatrical Native American greeting
jokes.explanation: Establishes Ken's character but borders on stereotypical.

jokes.text: During the battle, 93-year-old chief Wakote was shot 102 times by the calvary. Did he die? Yes.
jokes.setup: Ken describing the brutal historical battle
jokes.punchline: Ben's naive question and Ken's matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works because of Ben's innocent question and Ken's deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: The atrocities are in blue.
jokes.setup: Leslie showing a map of historical atrocities
jokes.punchline: The casual presentation of horrific events as color-coded data
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that shows Leslie's bureaucratic approach to sensitive topics.

jokes.text: There are two things I know about white people. They love Matchbox 20, and they are terrified of curses.
jokes.setup: Ken speaking to the camera in confessional style
jokes.punchline: The specific and unexpected pairing of Matchbox 20 with curse fear
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational comedy with perfect specificity. The Matchbox 20 reference is unexpected and quotable.

jokes.text: Take that, curse! / Take that, curse! / Ouch, ouch, ouch! Right in the chest, ooh! Ow, ow, ow! Bazooka!
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben celebrating getting more cotton candy machines
jokes.punchline: Their elaborate pretend combat with the curse
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that shows the characters' playful dynamic but goes on a bit long.

jokes.text: She's like an eagle-eyed tiger. / Eagle-eyed tiger! New band name, I call it!
jokes.setup: Leslie describing Joan Callamezzo
jokes.punchline: Andy's immediate excitement about the band name potential
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy moment that calls back to his running gag of finding band names everywhere.

jokes.text: But he did it so nicely that I didn't even realize he did it. / I've done that to multiple men.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining how Chris broke up with her
jokes.punchline: April's casual admission of doing the same manipulative thing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for April that reveals her manipulative side in a darkly funny way.

jokes.text: Two days ago I was sobbing at a pizza buffet and they asked me to leave. I've been looking at some dog adoption web sites. Bought $700 worth of candles from Anthropologie. I did this to my hair.
jokes.setup: Ann describing how she's handling the breakup
jokes.punchline: The escalating list of increasingly pathetic post-breakup behaviors
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list that builds to the hair reveal. Relatable post-breakup spiral.

jokes.text: Normally, people tell you to talk about your problems, I'm gonna recommend you bottle that noise up. / That's what my mailman said.
jokes.setup: Ron giving Ann relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Ann's casual revelation that her mailman gave the same advice
jokes.explanation: Good Ron advice moment with Ann's unexpected addition about the mailman.

jokes.text: Muah-muah-murder. Murdering the most melons. Shaquille showered shame on Shakira's sheets.
jokes.setup: Jerry doing vocal exercises
jokes.punchline: The absurd alliterative phrases, especially the Shaquille/Shakira one
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry moment with increasingly absurd tongue twisters.

jokes.text: But deadly. When was the last time this giant wheel of death was inspected?
jokes.setup: Leslie showing Joan the beautiful ferris wheel
jokes.punchline: Joan immediately focusing on danger and calling it a 'wheel of death'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joan Callamezzo moment - immediately finding the negative angle. 'Wheel of death' is quotable.

jokes.text: How many of these carnies are illegals?
jokes.setup: Joan doing her investigative reporting
jokes.punchline: The blunt, inappropriate question delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Shows Joan's inappropriate sensationalism but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: Are you [bleep] kidding me? You got Li'l Sebastian?
jokes.setup: Joan has been cynical about everything at the festival
jokes.punchline: Her complete change in attitude when she sees Little Sebastian
jokes.explanation: Great payoff showing even the cynical Joan is impressed by Little Sebastian.

jokes.text: Thank you, Joan. Li'l Sebastian isn't bad either.
jokes.setup: Joan compliments Little Sebastian saying he's adorable
jokes.punchline: Tom's assumption she was complimenting him
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom vanity moment but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Did you get your breasts done? You look amazing. / Yes, thanks for noticing.
jokes.setup: Tom talking to Joan Callamezzo
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate personal comment and Joan's casual acceptance
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor showing both characters' inappropriate boundaries.

jokes.text: And when it does, I'm gonna be there to give you a foot massage... To completion.
jokes.setup: Tom promising Joan the festival will impress her
jokes.punchline: The increasingly inappropriate offer ending with 'to completion'
jokes.explanation: Tom's inappropriate escalation lands with uncomfortable humor.

jokes.text: Well, I want a billion Teddy bears. / Well, that's a little unrealistic. This is a hard game. Two million.
jokes.setup: Andy promising to win April teddy bears at carnival games
jokes.punchline: Andy's serious negotiation of impossible numbers as if they're achievable
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's optimism and April's testing of it.

jokes.text: Hey, I love you. / Dude, shut up! / That is awesome sauce!
jokes.setup: Andy declares his love for April
jokes.punchline: April's harsh rejection followed by Andy's enthusiasm about 'awesome sauce'
jokes.explanation: Important character moment that sets up the relationship tension.

jokes.text: Wow, that is a shockingly huge mini horse erection.
jokes.setup: Tom looking at Little Sebastian
jokes.punchline: The blunt observation about the horse's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that relies on shock value rather than cleverness.

jokes.text: Well, uh, you know, when I was an 18-year-old mayor, I tried to build a winter sports complex called Ice Town and it bankrupted the city.
jokes.setup: Joan asking Ben about his failures
jokes.punchline: The absurd backstory of an 18-year-old mayor building 'Ice Town'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character backstory that's both absurd and specific. 'Ice Town' is highly quotable.

jokes.text: I was also short-stop on my JV baseball team. No one ever brings that up.
jokes.setup: Ben listing his past roles after the Ice Town disaster
jokes.punchline: His disappointed tone about people not mentioning his baseball position
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment showing his need for recognition even for minor achievements.

jokes.text: Uh, somebody left the gate open and he got out, Jerry! / What are you talking about? / Unbelievable. Another in a long string of miserable failures. You lost Li'l Sebastian! / I wasn't even here! / Exactly.
jokes.setup: Little Sebastian is missing
jokes.punchline: Tom blaming Jerry and then using Jerry's defense as further proof of guilt
jokes.explanation: Great example of Tom's blame-shifting and illogical arguments against Jerry.

jokes.text: Let's go ahead, let's get some shots of chipped paint and crying babies. How about some of those spooky traffic lights.
jokes.setup: Joan realizes there's a scandal brewing
jokes.punchline: Her immediate shift to gathering negative footage, including 'spooky traffic lights'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joan moment showing her sensationalist approach. 'Spooky traffic lights' is absurd.

jokes.text: Plus we lost that little pony. / It's a mini horse! / And that's the third lightning rod. Triple lightning rod! / And then you have the curse, quadruple lightning rod.
jokes.setup: Ben listing all the problems with the festival
jokes.punchline: The escalating 'lightning rod' metaphor and Leslie's correction about pony vs mini horse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback to the pony/mini horse distinction.

jokes.text: Oh, no. No. We all just behave rationally and believe we are all in charge of our own destinies. Come on.
jokes.setup: Ben asking if people really believe in curses
jokes.punchline: Leslie's sarcastic response revealing that yes, people do believe in curses
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic delivery that reveals the town's superstitious nature.

jokes.text: Maybe Li'l Sebastian got upset because he tried to share his true feelings with someone and they didn't reciprocate.
jokes.setup: Everyone wondering why Little Sebastian ran away
jokes.punchline: April projecting her relationship problems onto the horse
jokes.explanation: Clever character moment where April projects her Andy issues onto Little Sebastian.

jokes.text: Good idea. Horses love mazes, come on!
jokes.setup: Tom suggests checking the corn maze for Little Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Andy's confident but completely wrong assertion about horses loving mazes
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy moment of stating nonsense with complete confidence.

jokes.text: Ah, good. Anne, you're so beautiful. Beautiful Anne.
jokes.setup: Ann handles a situation well
jokes.punchline: Leslie's random compliment about Ann's beauty in the middle of crisis
jokes.explanation: Callback to Leslie's tendency to randomly compliment Ann but feels forced here.

jokes.text: Oh, I love these things. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Joan watching an animated recreation of events
jokes.punchline: Her unprofessional enjoyment of the sensationalized animation
jokes.explanation: Shows Joan's unprofessional enjoyment of sensationalism.

jokes.text: You know how Indians call corn maize? This is why. I never knew that.
jokes.setup: Andy and April are in the corn maze
jokes.punchline: Andy's completely wrong etymology about corn/maize
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment of confident ignorance with fake learning.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I'm gonna go look by myself. / Hey, are you all right? You seem super not happy. / I'm fine. / Oh, I forgot to tell you. I might be going to Venezuela tomorrow. Forever.
jokes.setup: April is clearly upset and Andy is trying to help
jokes.punchline: April's casual mention of leaving forever to Venezuela
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing April's dramatic tendencies and emotional manipulation.

jokes.text: I don't know what's sicker, me or your body. / You aren't sick. / Maybe you should check out my abs. / Are you experiencing abdominal pain? / Every day at the gym.
jokes.setup: Bodybuilder trying to flirt with Ann
jokes.punchline: Ann's medical interpretation of his flirting and his literal response about gym pain
jokes.explanation: Good miscommunication comedy with Ann's medical mindset clashing with flirtation.

jokes.text: Who said anything about a boyfriend? / Use him, abuse him, lose him.
jokes.setup: Ann saying the bodybuilder isn't boyfriend material
jokes.punchline: Donna's casual advice about using people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna advice that's both practical and morally questionable.

jokes.text: Yeah, Jerry. He's right here in my pocket.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if they found Little Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Tom's sarcastic response implying Jerry is an idiot
jokes.explanation: Standard Tom sarcasm toward Jerry but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: There are not, as one reporter suggested, dozens of escaped convicts meandering around the carnival. And, at no time was any Parks Department quote, 'feasting on petting zoo animals.'
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to dispel false rumors
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd rumors she has to deny, especially 'feasting on petting zoo animals'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurd rumors. 'Feasting on petting zoo animals' is highly quotable.

jokes.text: Leslie, how big of a disaster will this be, on a scale of 9 to 10?
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about potential disaster
jokes.punchline: The loaded scale that only allows for levels of disaster
jokes.explanation: Clever setup that reveals media bias by offering only disaster levels as options.

jokes.text: Yeah, the statement that this reporter has is a question. Will that be enough to lift the curse?
jokes.setup: Perd being called on at the press conference
jokes.punchline: His unnecessarily convoluted way of saying he has a question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Perd callback to his unnecessarily wordy reporting style. The meta-commentary on having a question is classically Perd.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Crap on a spatula.
jokes.setup: Something goes wrong at the worst possible moment
jokes.punchline: Leslie's unique, mild profanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie-specific mild profanity that's both clean and memorable.

jokes.text: Jerry's fault. Jerry's fault. Jerry's fault! Jerry's fault!
jokes.setup: Something goes wrong and people look for someone to blame
jokes.punchline: The immediate mob mentality of blaming Jerry
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag about Jerry being blamed for everything.

jokes.text: If they're missing this long, they're usually dead. / Well, if that's true, then you're gonna have to answer to the whole town. And God.
jokes.setup: Discussing how long Little Sebastian has been missing
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact statement about death and Tom's dramatic escalation
jokes.explanation: Good character moments - Ron's dark pragmatism and Tom's dramatic fear.

jokes.text: Andy, she's mad because you said 'awesome sauce' instead of, 'I love you, too.' April, he loves you. Stop being a child. Tom, you're clearly at fault here. Blaming Jerry won't save you. Jerry, we both know you were shotgunning funnel cakes instead of watching Li'l Sebastian. So everyone apologize to everyone else!
jokes.setup: Everyone is arguing and confused about their conflicts
jokes.punchline: Ron's efficient, harsh resolution of everyone's problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron moment where he cuts through everyone's drama with brutal efficiency and accuracy.

jokes.text: That's what makes the sauce so awesome.
jokes.setup: Andy and April reconciling after the 'awesome sauce' conflict
jokes.punchline: Andy's callback to the phrase that caused the problem, now used sweetly
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that resolves the relationship conflict while maintaining Andy's character voice.

jokes.text: Aw, Jerry just farted! I'm very upset, I get nervous.
jokes.setup: The group hug moment
jokes.punchline: Jerry's embarrassing bodily function and defensive explanation
jokes.explanation: Basic bathroom humor that fits Jerry's embarrassing character but isn't particularly clever. Relies on cheap physical comedy.

jokes.text: You single? / Not now, Ed.
jokes.setup: Leslie compliments Ed's technical knowledge about generators
jokes.punchline: Ed immediately trying to ask her out during the crisis
jokes.explanation: Good timing joke with Ed's inappropriate moment to flirt and Leslie's dismissal.

jokes.text: Much like the early settlers of Pawnee begged the Wamapoke 200 years ago. / Ah, but that was... / A trick. And over 50 Wamapoke were slaughtered.
jokes.setup: Leslie comparing her begging to historical precedent
jokes.punchline: The dark historical reality that Leslie casually acknowledges
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works through Leslie's casual acknowledgment of historical atrocities.

jokes.text: Beat it, Donna. / Aye, aye, captain.
jokes.setup: Bodybuilder tells Donna to leave so he can make out with Ann
jokes.punchline: Donna's mock-military salute acceptance
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Donna's easy-going attitude.

jokes.text: A native American tribe making a deal with the government. Heh! What could go wrong?
jokes.setup: Leslie asking Ken for help with the generator
jokes.punchline: Ken's sarcastic reference to the historical pattern of broken government promises
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor that references real historical patterns with perfect sarcasm and timing.

jokes.text: We spotted him from the top of the ferris wheel at the center of the corn maze. After the power came back on, we went and got him. It took us four hours to solve that maze. It took the horse 15 minutes. Jerry's still out there.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining how they found Little Sebastian
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Jerry is still lost in the maze while the horse escaped quickly
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes Jerry look worse than a horse at problem-solving.

jokes.text: I suppose I oughta go look for him. Right after I get a bratwurst.
jokes.setup: Ron realizes Jerry is still lost in the maze
jokes.punchline: His casual prioritization of food over Jerry's rescue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment showing his priorities and casual attitude toward Jerry's predicament.

jokes.text: Ice Town was a disaster. And it seems like it was probably your fault. From what I can tell, you mismanaged the hell out of it. / Wow, why even say that already?
jokes.setup: Ben apologizing for leaving during the crisis
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt assessment of Ice Town and Ben's hurt reaction
jokes.explanation: Good relationship moment showing Leslie's honesty and Ben's sensitivity about his past.

jokes.text: I don't get it. At all. It's kind of a small horse. I mean, what am I missing? Am I crazy? / Hey, Ben, you just missed it. He whinnied. / Oh, yeah, no. I totally heard that. It was great.
jokes.setup: Ben still doesn't understand the Little Sebastian obsession
jokes.punchline: Ben pretending to have heard the whinny to fit in
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Ben's continued confusion and his attempt to fake understanding.

tmdb_overview: It's make it or break it for the Parks Department at the Harvest Festival which is full of surprises.
insight: Event logistics comedy sustains 61 jokes despite middling 72.7 score, outpaced by darker ensemble work.
